title: IAB TCF consent string mutation without user interaction
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the legality of the initial consent; focuses on downstream mutation without an originating user interaction event.
evidence: Saved search 'tcf_consent_mutation' (hourly) emits sessions whose TCF v2.2 consent string changed between page impressions without a corresponding CMP interaction event.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.7(1) requires the controller to demonstrate consent; detecting silent string mutation is one part of that demonstration.
CPRA opt-out signals (GPC) can be misrepresented by server-side mutation; detection adds evidentiary weight.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.7(1) (parent assurance='partial'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An ad tag overwrites the TC string for a session without a CMP interaction event in the same window; the UC fires and names the tag and vendor.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A legitimate CMP interaction (user clicks 'Save preferences') legally changes the TC string; no alert.

dataSources: Web access logs capturing Set-Cookie: euconsent-v2, CMP interaction events (onetrust:cmp), client-side analytics.
spl:
index=web sourcetype=access_combined earliest=-1h euconsent_v2=*
| sort 0 session_id _time
| streamstats current=f last(euconsent_v2) as prior_consent by session_id
| where isnotnull(prior_consent) AND euconsent_v2!=prior_consent
| join type=outer session_id _time [search index=cmp sourcetype=onetrust:cmp action=interaction earliest=-1h | fields session_id _time | rename _time as interaction_time]
| where isnull(interaction_time) OR abs(_time-interaction_time)>60
| table _time session_id prior_consent euconsent_v2
description: Compares the TCF 2.2 consent string across consecutive page impressions in the same session and flags changes that occurred without a corresponding CMP interaction within 60 seconds. This is a known pattern of rogue ad-tech overriding user choice.
value: Catches a pattern that has surfaced repeatedly in EDPB enforcement decisions (Meta, Google ad-tech) before it becomes a supervisory finding.
implementation: (1) Preserve Set-Cookie headers in access logs; (2) ingest CMP interaction events; (3) run hourly; (4) on hit, freeze the vendor via ad-tech allow-list and open a DPO ticket.
visualization: Table of mutating sessions by vendor, bar chart of mutations per ad-tech partner, single-value 'sessions with genuine consent (24h)'.
knownFalsePositives: CMP token refreshes (same purpose mask, new expiry) can legitimately rewrite the TC string; allow-list based on purpose-bitmap equality.
